summary: Matched more of src/melee/if/ifprize.c by replacing placeholder call stubs with real prize-screen UI/control-flow logic, refining the main ifprize state struct, adding the OnEnter linked-list argument struct, storing created camera/light/UI/text objects in global state, and using inline-wrapper tricks to prevent an unwanted inline in un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
what_changed: Added include of placeholder.h, likely to support remaining unresolved placeholders or matching scaffolding in this file.
Introduced struct un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 with x0, x2, x4, and linked-list pointer x8, replacing the prior weaker arg struct shape near the OnEnter function.

Refined struct un_803F9D48 from broad padding and integer fields into typed fields: HSD_JObj*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_Text*, linked-list pointers, and an x30[3] array initialized to prize IDs 0x43, 0x44, 0x45.

Replaced the prior pragma dont_inline around un_802FE3F8 with static inline wrapper functions un_802FE3F8_inner and un_802FE3F8_noinline, then called the wrapper from un_802FEBE0_OnEnter to preserve matching behavior.

Implemented fn_802FE470 as a state machine over un_803F9D48.x1, including delay states, input handling via gm_801A36A0(4), linked-list advancement through x2C, cleanup of created GObjs and archive, and animation requests.

Updated un_802FE6A8 to save camera, light, UI gobj, JObj, and HSD_Text pointers into un_803F9D48, use & 0xFF on HSD_GObj object-class constants for matching, and scope GXColor/PAD_STACK near text setup.

Implemented un_802FE918 prize display behavior: audio selection, random non-repeating x3 selection, gmMainLib call, lookup through un_803F9B30/un_803F9A00, special handling for prize 0x3E, SisLib text updates, date/time formatting, language-dependent date order, and text drawing at 320/316.

Adjusted un_802FEBE0_OnEnter locals and initialization order, storing arg0 linked list state in un_803F9D48.x28/x2C, initializing x3 from HSD_Randi(3), setting state flags, computing animation/text index, and invoking un_802FE3F8_noinline.
smart_moves: Typed the global state struct progressively instead of leaving padding and raw ints, which made later gobj/text/jobj cleanup and animation code expressible and easier to verify.
Used small inline wrapper layers around un_802FE3F8 to control compiler inlining at a specific callsite without relying on a global pragma block.
Stored created camera, light, UI, and text objects in the persistent ifprize state so fn_802FE470 could accurately clean up and update them later.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack/layout behavior by scoping GXColor with PAD_STACK(0x14) inside a nested block and using casts such as HSD_GObj_804D784B & 0xFF.
Reconstructed real user-facing behavior instead of leaving dummy placeholder calls: prize text lookup, randomized audio, date formatting, and language-specific formatting were all restored.
decomp_lessons: When replacing a call-stub block, first refine the owning global state struct; typed fields often reveal lifecycle relationships between setup, per-frame callback, and teardown functions.
Linked-list argument structs should be modeled explicitly when OnEnter-style scene entry data chains through later UI updates.
For matching, local declaration order, nested scopes, casts to narrower object-kind values, and explicit PAD_STACK sizes can matter as much as high-level logic.
If a callee must remain out-of-line for one caller but normal pragmas are awkward or undesirable, an inline wrapper/noinline indirection pattern can shape the compiler output.
UI decomp work often couples scene object setup with SisLib text state; keep HSD_Text pointers in the same persistent struct as the gobj/jobj pointers if the frame callback mutates them.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions and globals retain address-based names such as un_802FE918, un_803F9D48, and un_804D6D98.
Temporary struct names encode their known use site, e.g. struct un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0.
Struct members remain offset-style names like x0a, x0b, x10, x28, and x30 when semantic names are not proven.
Helper wrappers use suffixes that explain matching intent, e.g. un_802FE3F8_inner and un_802FE3F8_noinline.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced #pragma dont_inline with a pair of static inline wrappers to keep un_802FE3F8 from being inlined into un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
Used explicit casts to short* when writing unsigned-short fields through un_802FE3F8, preserving the inferred field storage while satisfying the function signature.
Used HSD_GObj_804D784B & 0xFF and HSD_GObj_804D784A & 0xFF in object attachment calls, indicating the generated code needed byte-sized behavior.
Moved GXColor and PAD_STACK into a nested scope in un_802FE6A8 to shape stack allocation/lifetime.
Introduced local temporaries such as v_x8 and sp1C[0x104] to match data movement and stack layout around SisLib text/date formatting.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human_text_excerpt was empty, so no reviewer intent or discussion can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename un_803F9D48 fields or give semantic names to ifprize state members.
Search for other uses of un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 to confirm the linked-list argument layout and x2 semantics.
Compare other UI scene PRs that use inline wrapper tricks instead of #pragma dont_inline.
Look for later cleanup of placeholder.h in src/melee/if/ifprize.c.
Search for similar HSD_GObj_804D784A/B & 0xFF matching casts in other scene setup files.
